ports.name: clk_i
ports.description: Primary clock for the interrupt-file state.

ports.name: rst_ni
ports.description: Active-low reset.

ports.name: req_valid_i
ports.description: Request qualifier for the normalized local access port.

ports.name: req_write_i
ports.description: High for writes, low for reads.

ports.name: req_addr_i
ports.description: Normalized local address. 0x000/0x004 model seteipnum_le/be, 0x070/0x072/0x080-0x0FF model the interrupt-file registers, and 0x140 models topei claim/read semantics.

ports.name: req_wdata_i
ports.description: Write payload for the normalized local access port.

ports.name: rsp_valid_o
ports.description: Response qualifier for reads and accepted writes.

ports.name: rsp_rdata_o
ports.description: Read data from the normalized local access port.

ports.name: irq_o
ports.description: Hart-facing external interrupt signal controlled by eidelivery, eithreshold, eie, and eip.

notes: The staged public boundary is a normalized single-interrupt-file interface derived from AIA chapter 3, not a full hart CSR subsystem.
Reads from address 0x140 return the current top pending-and-enabled interrupt in AIA topei format; writes to 0x140 ignore wdata and claim that interrupt by clearing its pending bit.
Addresses not listed in the task README are reserved and must read as zero and ignore writes.

ports.name: clk_i
ports.description: Primary clock for the IDC state.

ports.name: rst_ni
ports.description: Active-low reset.

ports.name: domain_ie_i
ports.description: Normalized copy of APLIC domaincfg.IE for the owning domain.

ports.name: src_pending_i
ports.description: Pending bits for the 32-source normalized direct-delivery view. Source 0 is reserved and ignored.

ports.name: src_enable_i
ports.description: Enable bits for the same 32 normalized sources. Source 0 is reserved and ignored.

ports.name: src_prio_i
ports.description: Flattened 32 x 8-bit priority numbers, one byte per source. Smaller nonzero values mean higher priority.

ports.name: req_valid_i
ports.description: Request qualifier for the normalized IDC local access port.

ports.name: req_write_i
ports.description: High for writes, low for reads.

ports.name: req_addr_i
ports.description: Normalized IDC-local register address.

ports.name: req_wdata_i
ports.description: Write payload for the normalized IDC local access port.

ports.name: rsp_valid_o
ports.description: Response qualifier for reads and accepted writes.

ports.name: rsp_rdata_o
ports.description: Read data from the normalized IDC local access port.

ports.name: irq_o
ports.description: Hart-facing external interrupt signal for this IDC.

ports.name: claim_pulse_o
ports.description: One-cycle pulse when a read of claimi returns a nonzero winning source and requests that source to be claimed.

ports.name: claim_id_o
ports.description: Claimed source number when claim_pulse_o is asserted.

notes: The staged public boundary is a normalized single-hart APLIC IDC block, not a full APLIC domain.
The task normalizes the direct-delivery candidate set down to 32 sources numbered 1..31, with source 0 reserved and ignored.

The environment supplies per-source pending, enable, and priority inputs; the IDC computes topi and claimi locally, applies ithreshold, and emits claim_pulse_o/claim_id_o when claimi reads a nonzero winner.
Addresses not listed in the task README are reserved and must read as zero and ignore writes.
